scenario: Helios Insurance keeps a FEEL list of 3000 claims sorted by submission time inside a process variable and needs a gateway expression that reads the most recently submitted claim, which sits at the end of the list, in Camunda 8.8.
question: Which FEEL expression returns the last element of a list named claims regardless of its length?

options.text: Index claims with claims[0] so the engine reads the first slot of the list and returns the most recent submission record
Index claims with claims[count(claims)+1] so the engine reads past the end and returns the most recent submission record
Index claims with claims[length(claims)] so the engine treats the count as the trailing slot and returns the recent submission
Index claims with claims[-1] so the engine counts from the end of the list and returns the last element of the list

a.text: Incorrect. The FEEL list index starts at 1, not 0; the docs note that a[0] returns null because index 0 is outside the valid range of a list.

b.text: Incorrect. FEEL list indexing returns null when the index is out of range; adding one to the element count points past the last element and yields null.

c.text: Incorrect. The documented mechanism for the last element of a FEEL list is the negative index -1, which counts from the end; relying on a length-based positive index is not the documented pattern in the chunk.

d.text: Correct. FEEL negative indexes count from the end of the list, so a[-1] returns the last element per the language guide; for [1,2,3,4][-1] the result is 4.

explanation: FEEL list indexes start at 1 and negative indexes count from the end, so a[-1] returns the last element of list a; if the index is outside the range the result is null, including a[0].
